title: Affordable Murphy Bed
summary: A wall-mounted twin murphy bed built from a simple plywood-and-1x12 box and a hinged mattress panel, using standard door hinges instead of a purchased murphy-bed hardware kit.
description:
The whole cost-saving idea here is that the "mechanism" is just heavy-duty hinges, not a purchased hardware kit — which is honest about the tradeoff: a hinge swings freely, so a magnetic clasp to hold the bed shut is not optional, it's the only thing standing between a closed bed and one that swings open on its own. Compression (soft-close) hinges are offered as an upgrade to slow the fall rather than stop it outright, worth adding if the budget allows.

The mattress panel is really a shallow box of its own, built from the same plywood-and-board approach as the wall box, with a decorative door pattern applied to its face from thin hobby-stock trim. Installation is not optional finishing — the wall box must be screwed into wall studs at both top and bottom, since this piece is designed to carry a mattress and hardware load hanging off a wall, not just sit against it.

tools.note: Cuts the diagonal mattress-box side pieces; a circular saw also works.

materials.name: Heavy-duty hinges or continuous piano hinge
Compression hinges
materials.note: Optional; slows the bed lowering into place.

cutList.note: Cut lengthwise at a diagonal from one board to get both sides.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Fasten the sides to the bottom with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Add the back supports
steps.body: Fasten the back supports to the box, flush with the top and back, using 2" screws and glue. Also fasten through the bottom of the box into the bottom support.

steps.title: Add the front support and side trim
steps.body: Fasten the front support trim to the box with 2" screws and glue, top edges flush. Using 2" nails and glue, fasten the side trim to the box with outside edges flush — also fasten to the bottom of the box, and fasten through the top trim into the top end edge of the side trim.

steps.title: Add the front top trim
steps.body: Fasten the front top trim in place with 1-1/4" nails and glue, keeping top and outside edges flush. Use 2" nails at the ends to fasten to the side trim and sides.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Using 2" nails and glue, fasten the top back piece to the box top, leaving a 1" overhang at both ends and keeping the back flush. Attach the top front piece the same way, leaving a 1" overhang on the front and sides.

steps.title: Build the mattress box
steps.body: Screw the bottom board to the plywood back with 2" screws and glue. Fasten the diagonally-cut sides to the bottom and back with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Add the door decoration
steps.body: Using 1/4" hobby stock, fasten your desired decorative pattern to the face of the mattress box door. Secure with glue, 5/8" brad nails, and clamps, and let dry.

steps.title: Install the hardware
steps.body: Use heavy-duty hinges (or a continuous piano hinge) to attach the mattress box to the wall box. Add a magnetic clasp to keep the door from falling open when shut. Optional compression hinges keep the door from free-falling open when unlatched.
materials: Heavy-duty hinges or continuous piano hinge

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill any nail or screw holes with wood filler, sand, and finish as desired.

steps.title: Install on the wall
steps.body: Screw through the back-support boards into wall studs, at both the top and bottom of the box. A wide elastic strap secured to the plywood door is also recommended to keep the mattress in place when the bed is closed. Sized to fit a standard twin mattress, 39" x 75".

images.alt: Wall-mounted murphy bed with hinged mattress panel
